title: Patient Confidentiality and HIPAA: Protecting Health Information
subtitle: Across one shift the nurse faces a phone caller, family at the desk, hallway conversations, and a social-media temptation. The nurse must protect every patient's information while still sharing what the care team legitimately needs.

patient.name: R. Castillo
patient.admitDate: Medical unit, stable. Multiple people request information during the shift; the nurse must protect confidentiality while sharing only what the care team needs.

vitals.temp: Confirm patient permission; check code word
No PHI in public areas; lower voice
Never post patient info or photos

labs.v: No PHI in halls or elevators.
Never post patient info or photos.

nursesNote: R. Castillo, 57, is stable on a medical unit. During the shift several people request her information: a caller on the phone, family members at the desk, and staff chatting in the hallway. The nurse protects confidentiality by sharing protected health information (PHI) only with those involved in the patient's care and only the minimum necessary. Before releasing any information to a caller or visitor, the nurse verifies identity and confirms the patient's consent (or the designated code word). PHI is not discussed in public areas, devices are kept logged off, and nothing about any patient is posted on social media. The nurse knows the limited permitted disclosures (such as mandatory abuse reporting and certain communicable diseases) and reports any suspected breach promptly per facility policy.
providerOrders: Maintain confidentiality of all protected health information (PHI)
Share only the minimum necessary, and only with the care team
Verify identity and patient consent before releasing information to callers or visitors
Avoid PHI in public areas; never post patient information on social media
Report any suspected confidentiality breach promptly per facility policy

opts.text: Protected health information should be shared only with those involved in the patient's care
Disclose only the minimum necessary information for the purpose
Verify a caller's identity and the patient's consent before releasing information
Patient information may be discussed freely in elevators and hallways
It is acceptable to look up the chart of a patient you are not caring for if you are curious
Posting any patient information or photos on social media is prohibited
A designated code word lets the patient control who may receive information
Computers should be logged off and written records secured when unattended
Some disclosures are permitted by law, such as mandatory abuse reporting
Sharing your login so a coworker can chart quickly is acceptable
Family members are automatically entitled to all of a competent adult's information
A suspected breach should be reported promptly per facility policy

steps.title: Prioritize Hypotheses
steps.sub: A caller asks for the patient's information — order the steps

opts.text: Verify the identity of the person requesting information
Confirm the patient has authorized sharing (consent or code word)
If not authorized, do not disclose; refer the request to the patient
If authorized, share only the minimum necessary information
Document what was shared and with whom
Give shift report in a private area, sharing only what the next nurse needs
Verify a caller's identity and the patient's consent before sharing
Use the patient's code word to confirm who may receive updates
Log off the computer and secure papers before stepping away
Report a communicable disease to public health as required by law
Discuss the patient's diagnosis loudly at the nurses' station near visitors
Post an 'anonymous' patient story with photos on social media
Look up a neighbor's chart out of curiosity
Tell a caller details without confirming consent

steps.title: Take Action
steps.sub: Sequence the safeguards during the shift
steps.inst: Rank the order of actions. #1 = FIRST.

opts.text: Give report privately, sharing only what is needed
Verify identity and consent before releasing any information
Speak quietly and keep PHI out of public areas
Log off devices and secure written records
Report any suspected breach promptly per policy
The nurse confirms the code word before updating a family member
Only the minimum necessary information is shared with the care team
A communicable disease is reported to public health as required
The computer is logged off and charts are secured when unattended
The nurse posts a patient photo on social media
Patient details are given to a caller without verifying consent
The nurse browses a coworker's chart out of curiosity
The diagnosis is discussed loudly in a crowded elevator
